name: Everest Base Camp Trek
permit: Sagarmatha National Park Permit and Khumbu Rural Municipality Permit
summary: A classic high-altitude Himalayan route through Sherpa settlements, monasteries, glaciers and Everest viewpoints.
guidance: Acclimatize at Namche Bazaar and Dingboche.
Start walking early because clouds often build after noon.
Keep one spare weather day for Lukla flights.

name: Annapurna Base Camp Trek
summary: A scenic route from terraced hills and Gurung villages to the Annapurna Sanctuary amphitheatre.
guidance: Use trekking poles for long stone stair sections.
Respect conservation rules inside ACAP.
Avoid avalanche-prone sections during heavy winter snow.

name: Langtang Valley Trek
permit: Langtang National Park Permit and TIMS Card
summary: A culturally rich valley trek north of Kathmandu with forests, yak pastures and close Himalayan views.
guidance: Spend a rest day near Kyanjin Gompa.
Road travel to Syabrubesi can be slow in monsoon.
Support local lodges rebuilt after the 2015 earthquake.

name: Mardi Himal Trek
summary: A shorter ridge trek near Pokhara with rhododendron forest and close views of Machhapuchhre.
guidance: Good for travellers with limited time.
Book lodges early in peak season.
Avoid exposed ridges during thunderstorms.

name: Ghorepani Poon Hill Trek
summary: A beginner-friendly trek famous for sunrise views over Dhaulagiri and Annapurna.
guidance: Start before sunrise for Poon Hill.
Spring has strong rhododendron bloom.
Works well as a short trekking package.

name: Manaslu Circuit Trek
permit: Manaslu Restricted Area Permit, MCAP and ACAP
summary: A remote circuit around Manaslu crossing Larkya La with strong cultural diversity.
guidance: A registered guide is required.
Plan carefully around Larkya La snow conditions.
Carry enough cash because ATM access is limited.

name: Upper Mustang Trek
permit: Upper Mustang Restricted Area Permit and ACAP
summary: A trans-Himalayan route through dry cliffs, caves, monasteries and Tibetan-influenced settlements.
guidance: Restricted area permit is mandatory.
Wind is strong after midday.
Carry sun protection because the route is dry and open.

name: Gokyo Lakes Trek
permit: Sagarmatha National Park Permit and Khumbu Rural Municipality Permit
summary: A high mountain route to turquoise lakes and Gokyo Ri viewpoint above the Ngozumpa Glacier.
guidance: Add acclimatization at Namche.
Check snow conditions near Renjo La if extending.
Drink enough water at altitude.

name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp Trek
permit: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit and KCAP
summary: A remote eastern Nepal route to the base of Kanchenjunga through forests, villages and alpine valleys.
guidance: Travel with a guide because it is a restricted area.
Plan food and lodging carefully.
Allow extra days for remote access.

name: Makalu Base Camp Trek
summary: A rugged wilderness trek into Makalu Barun National Park with deep valleys and high alpine terrain.
guidance: Prepare for remote sections with fewer lodges.
Weather can change quickly near base camp.
Carry basic repair and medical supplies.

name: Rara Lake Trek
summary: A western Nepal trek to the country's largest lake with forested hills and quiet villages.
guidance: Flights can be weather dependent.
Carry warm clothes for cold nights.
Respect national park rules near the lake.

name: Khopra Danda Trek
summary: A community lodge trek with panoramic Dhaulagiri and Annapurna views from Khopra Ridge.
guidance: Use community lodges to support local income.
Side trip to Khayer Lake requires an early start.
Ridge weather can be windy.

name: Helambu Trek
permit: Shivapuri Nagarjun and Langtang National Park Permits
summary: A short trek close to Kathmandu with Tamang and Hyolmo villages, forests and ridge views.
guidance: Good for a short semester field visit.
Carry rain protection in monsoon.
Use local homestays where possible.

name: Tamang Heritage Trail
summary: A cultural trekking trail through Tamang settlements, hot springs, monasteries and mountain viewpoints.
guidance: Choose homestays for cultural learning.
Ask permission before photographing people.
Hot spring stops need extra time.

name: Shey Phoksundo Lake Trek
summary: A western Nepal route to a deep blue alpine lake, waterfalls and Bon cultural villages.
guidance: Flights to Juphal may be delayed.
Stay on marked paths near steep lake cliffs.
Carry cash for remote villages.

name: Pikey Peak Trek
summary: A lower Everest region trek with sunrise views of Everest, Numbur and surrounding Himalayan ranges.
guidance: Good alternative when Lukla flights are uncertain.
Pack warm layers for the viewpoint.
Road access is improving but still slow.

name: Nar Phu Valley Trek
permit: Nar Phu Restricted Area Permit and ACAP
summary: A restricted-area trek into remote stone villages and high passes north of the Annapurna Circuit.
guidance: Guide and restricted permit are required.
Acclimatize before Kang La.
Snow can block the pass in winter.

name: Tilicho Lake Trek
summary: A high-altitude Annapurna side route to one of Nepal's most famous mountain lakes.
guidance: Cross landslide areas carefully.
Acclimatize in Manang.
Start early for the lake day.

name: Api Base Camp Trek
summary: A far-western wilderness trek towards Api Himal with remote villages, forests and alpine pastures.
guidance: Expect basic lodges and camping-style sections.
Hire local support for navigation.
Allow extra travel time from Kathmandu.

name: Dhaulagiri Circuit Trek
summary: A demanding circuit route crossing French Pass and Dhampus Pass around Dhaulagiri.
guidance: Suitable for experienced trekkers.
Camping logistics may be needed.
Check snow and pass conditions carefully.

name: Royal Trek
permit: No special permit for most sections
summary: A gentle low-altitude trek near Pokhara with village scenery, lakes and Annapurna views.
guidance: Ideal for beginner trekkers.
Can be combined with Pokhara city mapping.
Carry enough water in warm months.
